title: Ethical Transition, Rejection Learning, and Continuing Career Development · Career Evidence Case: Transition Integrity
summary: After an outcome, the candidate either stops learning, extracts certainty from sparse feedback, burns relationships, or carries confidential material into the next role. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transition integrity increment in first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
Separate direct feedback, process outcome, missing signal, candidate inference, repeated pattern, and uncontrollable variance before changing strategy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Transition Integrity · Theory Transition Integrity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transition Integrity contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Transition Integrity · Theory Transition Integrity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed. For Predict Transition Integrity · Theory Transition Integrity, keep Transition Integrity connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

options.text: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Portfolio value justifies retaining prior employer material. That claim fails the predict transition integrity evidence boundary for Transition Integrity.

For predict transition integrity, apply Transition Integrity without its stated preconditions, omit the first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Transition Integrity for first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan.

content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A candidate interprets sparse rejection as a skill verdict, retains confidential work, closes relationships poorly, and enters a new role without an onboarding evidence plan. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed. content.code:
# Worked focus: career-transition-integrity — Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
focus: career-transition-integrity
decision-evidence: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Portfolio value justifies retaining prior employer material. This fails the Transition Integrity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
